examples.title: Truth Table Generator for 2-Input Gates
examples.description: Generates truth tables for basic 2-input logic gates
examples.code:
# Truth Table Generator for 2-Input Logic Gates
def generate_truth_table():
    print("A B | AND OR NAND NOR XOR")
    print("-" * 30)
    
    for A in [0, 1]:
        for B in [0, 1]:
            and_out = A and B
            or_out = A or B
            nand_out = not (A and B)
            nor_out = not (A or B)
            xor_out = A != B
            print(f"{A} {B} |  {int(and_out)}  {int(or_out)}   {int(nand_out)}   {int(nor_out)}   {int(xor_out)}")

generate_truth_table()
examples.explanation: This Python code generates a truth table showing outputs of basic 2-input logic gates for all possible input combinations (00, 01, 10, 11).

examples.title: Binary to Decimal Conversion
examples.description: Converts binary number to decimal equivalent
examples.explanation: This C program converts a binary number (entered as digits) to its decimal equivalent by extracting each bit and multiplying by its positional weight (powers of 2).

examples.title: Simple 2-to-1 Multiplexer in Verilog
examples.description: Behavioral description of a 2-to-1 multiplexer using Verilog HDL
examples.explanation: This Verilog code describes a 2-to-1 multiplexer at behavioral level. The output Y selects input A when select=0, and input B when select=1. The testbench verifies all input combinations.

examples.title: Boolean Function Implementation Using Gates
examples.description: Implements a Boolean function F = A.B + A.C using basic gates
examples.explanation: This code implements the Boolean function F = A.B + A.C using basic AND and OR gates. It generates a truth table showing the function output for all 8 input combinations.
